template.name: Modern Coastal Twilight
template.author: Halo Films
template.description: Twelve-slot walkthrough graded for blue-hour / twilight on a modern coastal property. Architecture preservation is hard-constrained — the look comes entirely from a colorist's pass: cool ocean-blue shadows, dusty cyan midtones, soft salmon-and-amber highlight rolloff that reads as just-after-sunset. Same scene structure as cinematic-editorial-v1 so existing project photos slot in directly; only the visual style differs.

photo_requirement.intent: Front of the house, full façade visible. Establishing shot.
composition_hints: Stand 25–40 ft from the front of the house
Center the front door horizontally; keep camera level
Get the full roofline and a sliver of foreground in frame

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture and landscape EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, roofline, gable, column, fixture, plant, vehicle, or structural element.
• Do not invent any feature not visible in the source photo.
• If the source has no visible interior light through windows, do NOT introduce one.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight (blue hour):
• Re-grade as just-after-sunset blue hour. Sky shifts to a graduated salmon-to-deep-cyan band; preserve any cloud structure exactly.
• Cool dusty-blue shadows, slightly desaturated midtones, soft amber highlight rolloff on west-facing surfaces only.
• If the source has window panes, leave them untouched in shape but subtly warm any already-visible interior light to a soft tungsten amber. Do not introduce new lit windows.
• Slight cyan haze in the background — atmospheric perspective, not fog.
• Preserve all material textures (stucco, wood siding, metal trim) and white-balance the whites toward a neutral cool, never blown.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Treat this as a colorist's twilight grade over a real architectural photograph.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle forward push at twilight. Depth-reprojected from the source.

photo_requirement.intent: Looking from the front door inward, or from inside the entry. Establishes the threshold.
composition_hints: Stand at the front door looking in (or just inside looking deeper)
Center the strongest leading line — usually a hallway or sightline
Open all visible doors

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, fixture, wall, ceiling detail, floor material, or piece of furniture.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Calm, breeze-of-the-ocean interior grade. Cool dusty-cyan shadows, gently lifted midtones with a soft seafoam undertone, neutral whites.
• If practical lamps or sconces are already on in the source, warm their glow slightly toward soft tungsten — do not introduce new ones.
• Slight cool tint in any visible window light, as if the sky outside is the deep blue of just-after-sunset.
• Preserve all material textures (driftwood, linen, stone, plaster) — do not soften or smooth them.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Colorist's twilight grade over a real interior photograph.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle forward push through the entry. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Wide kitchen shot showing the island or main run of cabinetry.
composition_hints: Stand at one corner of the kitchen, capture the full opposite wall
Get the full counter or island in frame
Clear the counters of clutter

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, cabinet, drawer, appliance, fixture, light, countertop edge, or piece of decor.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source. If the source has no window, do NOT introduce one to motivate an ocean-view look.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.
• Cabinet fronts, hardware, and counter materials must remain identical in finish and color family.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Cool, breezy kitchen grade lit as if blue-hour ambient is filling the room.
• Slight desaturation of warm cabinetry colors toward a sun-bleached driftwood family.
• Cool blue-cyan shadow tint, neutral midtones, soft amber spill ONLY where a practical light is already on.
• If a window is in frame, the sky beyond reads as deep dusty-blue with a single warm horizon band — never bright daylight.
• Preserve material textures: stone counter veining, wood grain, brushed metal hardware.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade over a real kitchen photograph.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle lateral parallax through the kitchen at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Wide living-room shot showing the main seating arrangement.
composition_hints: Stand at the entry to the room, capture the full seating area
Center the focal point if there's a fireplace or hero window
Capture full ceiling height if possible

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, fireplace, mantel, ceiling beam, light fixture, piece of furniture, art, or decor.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Quiet, cinematic living-room grade for the 'last light of the day' feeling.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows; gently lifted midtones with a faint seafoam-green bias; controlled highlights.
• If the source has any window in frame, the sky outside should read as a soft graduated band from salmon at the horizon to deep cyan at the top — but only filling the existing window shape, never adding a new one.
• If practical lamps are already on, warm them toward a low-wattage tungsten glow.
• Preserve fabric textures (linen weave, woven throws, jute rugs) and any wood/stone grain.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Colorist's twilight grade over a real living-room photograph.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle lateral parallax through the living room at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Dining area with the table as focal point.
composition_hints: Stand at one end of the dining space, look down the table
If a chandelier or light fixture is present, frame it
Capture full ceiling height if possible

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, light fixture, chair, table, art, or decor.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Intimate, just-before-dinner-by-the-water dining grade.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows, neutral whites, slightly desaturated wood and fabric tones.
• If a chandelier or pendant is already on in the source, warm its glow softly to tungsten amber. Do not introduce new fixtures or new lit candles.
• Preserve material textures (linen tablecloths, woven seats, wood grain, ceramic).
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade over a real photograph.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked forward push through the dining space at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Same source as kitchen wide. Two-pass: twilight enhance, then 85mm detail recompose.
composition_hints: Reuse the same wide kitchen photo used for slot 03
Source must include an island or counter corner with sink or feature visible

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, cabinet, drawer, appliance, fixture, light, countertop edge, or piece of decor.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Same blue-hour kitchen treatment as the wide pass — cool dusty-cyan shadows, sun-bleached driftwood midtones, soft amber spill only on already-lit fixtures.
• Preserve stone, wood, and metal textures crisply.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade over a real kitchen photograph.

image_pipeline.label: recompose_detail
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:
• Preserve all materials, colors, white balance, and lighting direction from the source image.
• Do not invent any feature not visible in the source. Stone, wood, metal, and ceramic must remain the exact textures and colors of the source.

RECOMPOSE:
• Tight 85mm close-up of the kitchen island corner — countertop edge, sink, faucet, cabinetry beneath.
• Shallow depth of field with cinematic bokeh on the background.
• Sharp texture detail in stone countertop, wood cabinetry, hardware.
• Carry forward the cool dusty-cyan twilight shadows and any practical-light amber from the source — do not regrade.
• Photorealistic editorial style, balanced exposure with deep but not crushed shadows.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked near-static parallax on the recomposed 85mm detail. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Primary bedroom from the doorway or a corner.
composition_hints: Stand at the bedroom doorway looking in
Center the bed if possible
Capture at least one window for natural light

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, headboard, fixture, art, lamp, or piece of furniture.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Calm, breezy bedroom grade for the end of the day.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows, soft cream-and-linen midtones, gently desaturated wood tones.
• Any visible window: sky outside reads as a soft graduated salmon-to-cyan band, but only inside the existing window glass area.
• If a bedside lamp is already on, warm it softly to tungsten — do not introduce new lamps.
• Preserve linen, fabric, and wood textures.
• Subtle film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade — tranquil, polished, ocean-adjacent.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle lateral parallax through the bedroom at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Primary bathroom — vanity, tub, or shower. Tight detail framing.
composition_hints: Get close — this is a detail shot, not a wide
Capture stone, tile, or wood-grain texture
Look for natural light hitting a surface

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, faucet, cabinet, mirror, light fixture, tile, or fitting.
• Do not add light sources, windows, or openings that aren't already in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Cool, spa-feeling bath grade — dusty-cyan shadows, neutral porcelain whites, slightly desaturated stone and wood.
• Soft directional shadow shaping ONLY on surfaces already visible.
• If a vanity sconce is already on, warm it softly to low tungsten.
• Preserve stone, tile, metal, and ceramic textures with crisp detail.
• Subtle film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade.

image_pipeline.label: recompose_detail
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:
• Preserve all materials, colors, white balance, and lighting direction from the source image.
• Do not invent any feature not visible in the source.

RECOMPOSE:
• 85mm close-up of bathroom cabinetry, vanity surface, or tile detail.
• Shallow depth of field with cinematic bokeh on the background.
• Sharp texture detail in stone, wood grain, metal hardware.
• Carry forward the cool twilight shadows and any practical-light amber from the source — do not regrade.
• Photorealistic editorial style, balanced exposure with deep but not crushed shadows.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked near-static parallax on the recomposed 85mm bath detail. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Backyard or garden showing landscaping and any hardscape.
composition_hints: Stand at the edge of the yard, look across
Frame so the house is partially visible at one edge
Capture any landscape feature

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:
• Preserve the landscape and architecture EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any structure, plant, tree, hardscape, or furnishing.
• Do not invent any element not visible in the source.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Blue-hour outdoor grade. Sky shifts to a soft graduated salmon-to-deep-cyan; preserve cloud structure exactly.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows in foliage; muted seafoam-toward-olive greens, never lush mid-day saturation.
• Slight cool atmospheric haze in the background — depth, not fog.
• If garden lights or a pool light are already on in the source, warm them softly. Do not introduce new lights.
• Preserve hardscape texture (stone, concrete, wood).
• Subtle film grain.
• Photorealistic editorial twilight style.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked forward push through the outdoor space at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

composition_hints: Stand at one end of the pool, look down its length
Frame the house architecture as background
Catch reflections on the water

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:
• Preserve the landscape, architecture, and pool EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any structure, plant, fixture, lounge chair, or piece of decor.
• Do not invent any element not visible in the source.
• Water surface and reflections must remain consistent with the source pose.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight (signature shot):
• Hero blue-hour pool grade. Sky reads as soft graduated salmon-to-deep-cyan above; pool water deepens to a luminous cyan-teal but ONLY where water already exists.
• Pool reflections preserved — just shifted into the cooler twilight palette.
• If pool lights are already on, let them glow as soft warm amber pools beneath the surface. Do not introduce new lights.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows on hardscape, slightly desaturated greens in any planting, controlled highlight rolloff.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic editorial twilight style — the kind of frame a real-estate film closes on.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked forward push toward the pool at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Patio or covered outdoor living area.
composition_hints: Stand at the edge of the patio, look across
Frame any covered architecture (pergola, eave, ceiling)
Catch any practical fixtures

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:
• Preserve the architecture and furnishings EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any structure, plant, fixture, or piece of furniture.
• Do not invent any element not visible in the source.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight:
• Blue-hour patio grade. Cool dusty-cyan shadows, muted seafoam greens, soft amber spill only where a practical fixture already glows.
• If an outdoor pendant, fire feature, or string lights are already lit in the source, push them to a warm tungsten glow. Never add new ones.
• Sky preserved if visible — soft salmon-to-cyan band, never bright daylight.
• Subtle film grain.
• Photorealistic editorial twilight style.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked forward push through the patio at twilight. Depth-reprojected.

photo_requirement.intent: Closing exterior shot. Different exterior photo than slot 01 if multiple exist.
composition_hints: Prefer a photo with sky in frame
If only one exterior photo is available, this slot will be inactive

image_pipeline.label: twilight_enhance
image_pipeline.prompt:
ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS — these override every style direction below:
• Preserve the architecture and landscape EXACTLY. Do not add, remove, or move any window, door, roofline, gable, column, fixture, plant, or vehicle.
• Do not invent any feature not visible in the source photo.
• The output is the SAME scene with adjusted color and tone — not a reimagining.

STYLE DIRECTION — Modern Coastal Twilight (closing frame):
• Deeper blue-hour pull-back grade. Sky leans further toward deep cyan with a thinner salmon horizon than slot 01 — visually distinct as 'a beat later in the evening'.
• If interior windows are visible, allow already-on lights to glow softly warm so the house reads as inhabited at dusk. Never add new lit windows.
• Cool dusty-cyan shadows, controlled highlights, gentle film-grade rolloff.
• Preserve all material textures.
• Subtle 35mm film grain.
• Photorealistic. Twilight colorist's grade — quiet, finished, end-of-tour.

video_pass.prompt: Architecture-locked subtle pull-back at twilight. Depth-reprojected.
